notes: IEEE 1584-2018 incident-energy model -> NFPA 70E PPE category. Inputs: v_oc (V), i_bf_kA (kA), gap_mm (mm), electrode config, enclosure_mm (HxWxD mm), working_distance_mm (mm), arc_time_s (s).
Tolerances: incident energy +/-0.05 cal/cm2; arc-flash boundary +/-10 mm; PPE category EXACT (no tolerance).
Reference time = 0.2 s; bare-skin boundary energy Eb = 1.2 cal/cm2 (5.0 J/cm2).

Worked-example sources cited per case in the 'why' field. The k1..k13 / x IEEE 1584-2018 coefficient block is PROVISIONAL (VCB anchored to AF-1/AF-2; other configs unverified) pending owner verification against the licensed standard (Plan 11-02 Task 2 checkpoint resolved 'provisional + caveat + todo').

PPE category note: both AF-1 (0.85 cal/cm2) and AF-2 (0.62 cal/cm2) fall BELOW the 1.2 cal/cm2 NFPA 70E PPE floor, so the strict incident-energy method maps both to category 0 (no arc-rated PPE listed). The elek.com source labels these 'Cat 1'; that looser label conflicts with the strict NFPA 70E 1.2-threshold method this engine implements (research PPE table: <1.2 -> no AF PPE). expected_ppe_category is therefore 0 (defensible threshold mapping wins over the source's label).

Range-invalid / must-raise cases (AF-3: v_oc 50 V below 208 floor, v_oc 20 kV above 15 kV ceiling, negative gap) live in test_arcflash.py per D-03 (raise ArcFlashInputError, never clamp), not in this fixture.

cases.why: elek.com IEEE 1584-2018 worked example -- main switchboard (Iarc ~5.07 kA). 0.85 cal/cm2 < 1.2 floor -> NFPA 70E category 0 (source labels 'Cat 1'; strict threshold method governs).
elek.com IEEE 1584-2018 worked example -- distribution switchboard (Iarc ~3.885 kA). 0.62 cal/cm2 < 1.2 floor -> NFPA 70E category 0 (source labels 'Cat 1'; strict threshold method governs).
